text.value: ASK: What is 's date of birth?
ASK: Do you know 's exact date of birth?
ASK: What is their family name?
ASK: What is their first name?
Now, please enter information for the other members of the kaya.
It's important to try and get more exact age information for children. You can enter the child's date of birth as the first of the month in which they were born.
SAY: For children under 5, I'd like to try and get some more specific information about their age. Can you try and remember the year and month when was born?
ASK: In what year was born?
CHV attempted to register household by speaking to appropriate household member
Other means of travel to household

Now, approach the household. Introduce yourself and ask how many families (kayas) are living in this house. If more than one, ask if all heads of families are available and ready to speak at this time (Mhusi ka mkuu wa hii nyumba yupo?). If one head of family is away or not available to speak, or the heads of the families are not ready to be on the same conversation, speak with each family separately.
Is at least one head of family available to speak at this time?

Since the head of the family is not available to speak to you now, you can move on to the next household. We'll add a reminder to your task list to return to this household in a few days to finish the registration process.
Method to select date of birth - date or year of birth
_The registration process for this kaya is now complete._

SAY: From this registration, I've seen that you have [NUMBER] child(ren) under 5 who can benefit from this national program. Therefore, I'm asking for your permission to come back and check up on all issues related to their health care, nutrition, and child development. I would be grateful for your permission.

SAY: I see that there are some women of reproductive age in your family. I'd like to ask your permission to return later to speak with them personally about issues related to health during pregnancy and childbirth.

SAY: For each person, I will ask some basic details about who they are, starting with the primary contact person for this household. Who should I list as the primary contact for this household, who will be the person I reach out to coordinate care for themselves and other members of this household? This person should be reachable by phone.

Ask the person to list the names of all people who live in the house, and count to ensure that the number they provided is correct. Long term visitors to the house, such as a woman returning home for delivery, should not be counted â only people who are permanent residents of the house.
ASK: How many people live in your Kaya?
Are and the correct District and Shehia for this household?
Make sure location services on your phone are turned on.

_The purpose of the following questions regarding the household location is to help you locate this house again when it is time to return for another visit to the people who live here. Enter the information that you will need to help you easily find this house again when you need to before you approach the household. What is the address of this household?_
How do you travel between your home and this home?
How many minutes does it take you to travel between this home and your home?
Calculated age must be between 12 and 100!
ASK: What is 's date of birth?
ASK: Do you know 's exact date of birth?
ASK: What is their family name?
ASK: What is the first name of the best contact person for your household?
Use one of the following formats: +255XXXXXXXXX or 0XXXXXXXXX.
ASK: What is the best contact phone number for ?
You can leave this blank if there is only one good contact number for this household
Use one of the following formats: +255XXXXXXXXX or 0XXXXXXXXX.
ASK: is there another phone number used by or another member of the household?
ASK: In what year was born?
Contact ID of the logged in user
Facility ID for the parent user
Name of the logged in user
I need more time to decide
ASK: Are you willing to register your household?

You will proceed with registering one kaya at a time. Focus the conversation now to one head of family, to get their permission to register their family members. Once youâre done, start the registration process over for the other head of family.
SAY: At this point Iâd like to speak to each of you individually about getting some more information about the members of your family.
Discuss a good date for a follow up visit with the family, and enter the agreed upon date.

Your application will schedule a follow-up visit with this family in a few days to see if they are ready to be registered. If there are any other kayas living in this house, start a new household registration process and ask the other head of the family for permission to register the members of his family.
SAY: OK, I understand that you're not interested right now. I'll return in a few days to discuss this further.

Your application will not schedule any more visits to this family at this time. If they become interested at a later date you can always start providing services to them. If there are any other kayas living in this house, start a new household registration process and ask the other head of the family for permission to register the members of his family.
Thank the family for their time, and provide your contact information. Let them know that theyâre free to reach out at any time if they have questions about their health.
Once you've finished the conversation with the head of this kaya, remember to re-start a new household registration process for the other kaya(s) living in this house if you have not done so already.
SAY: I understand that you are not ready to register your family at this time. Could I return in a few days to discuss the services I can provide further?
ENTER: Is there more than one kaya living in this house?

Explain who you are and what your role as a community health volunteer is. Key points to mention include:
  • This is a nationwide effort implemented by the Ministry of Health to improve health services for communities in Zanzibar. Every area will now have a CHV who can care for families, pregnant women, and children up to age 5 in all issues related to their health care, nutrition, and child development.
  • At first, we will only register your household and ask some basic questions so I know how many people live in my area and what their health needs are. Every time there is a new client in the household, I will ask for their permission or their parentâs permission to visit with them and provide health services. Right now, my focus is on helping pregnant women to have healthy and safe pregnancies and deliveries, and make sure babies and young children are growing up healthy and strong. In the future, I may also be able to help more members of your family to live healthier lives.
  • Some of my roles as CHV will be to provide health education, screening for danger signs, give referrals when needed and follow up on them, I will make sure that your family get all the health information they need at the right time. Thank you.
  • Since am not a medical doctor/nurse, I will be using a smart phone to do my visits, this phone has a guide to help me do my work efficiently and also keep records of my visits. I can assure you that, our conversation remains confidential and will not be exposed to anyone/anywhere.
  • I also want to explain why itâs important for your family to enroll in this Program, your participation in the National CHV program would help the government to improve health services in our country, it will also provide you with an opportunity to learn your family health needs especially on pregnant/postpartum women and children and allow them to get the health care they need to improve your family welfare.
  • Allow the family to ask any questions they may have
Continue with the registration process for the people in this kaya.
Surface water (river, pond, dam, stream, lake)
Vendor provided (cart or truck) or bottled
Unimproved facility (open defecation, disposal with other forms of solid waste, pit latrine without slab/platform, open pit, hanging latrine, bucket latrine)
Shared facility (any facility shared with more than one household)
Improved facilities (sewer connections, septic system connections, pour-flush latrines, ventilated improved pit latrines and pit latrines with a slab or covered pit)
I need more time to decide

bind.calculate: if(selected( /data/child/sex_c ,"female") and ((((decimal-date-time(today()) - decimal-date-time( /data/child/date_of_birth_c )) div 365) >= 15 and ((decimal-date-time(today()) - decimal-date-time( /data/child/date_of_birth_c )) div 365) <= 49)),1,0)
